mission.title: Install and Configure Backstage on Kubernetes
mission.description: Backstage is an open framework for building developer portals, providing a centralized software catalog that helps teams manage their microservices and infrastructure effectively. Installing Backstage allows you to streamline your development environment and enhance productivity across your organization.

steps.title: Add the Backstage Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Backstage Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add backstage https://charts.backstage.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Backstage using Helm
steps.description:
Install Backstage in a new namespace called 'backstage' using the specified version.
```bash
helm install backstage backstage/backstage --version 1.48.3 --namespace backstage --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Backstage pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace backstage
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the Backstage deployment to set resource limits for the containers.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/backstage --namespace backstage --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

steps.title: Access Backstage
steps.description:
Port-forward the Backstage service to access it locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/backstage --namespace backstage 7007:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Backstage pods in a Running state when you check with `kubectl get pods`. You will also be able to access Backstage locally at http://localhost:7007.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add backstage https://charts.backstage.io
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the Backstage release using Helm to remove the deployment and associated resources from the cluster.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the 'backstage' namespace and verify that all resources have been cleaned up.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Backstage installation.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Helm repository and run the upgrade command to install the latest version of Backstage.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check the status of the Backstage pods to ensure that the upgrade was successful.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description: This issue occurs when a pod fails to start repeatedly. Check the logs of the pod to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description: If you cannot access Backstage, ensure that the service is running and that the port-forward command is set up correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources error
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter resource allocation errors, verify that your cluster has enough resources available for Backstage.

troubleshooting.title: Failed to pull image
troubleshooting.description: This error indicates that the container image cannot be pulled. Check your image repository credentials and network connectivity.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and have installed Helm and kubectl on your local machine.
